report_name: Global Cybersecurity Agencies Warn of FortiBleed Credential Exposure in Fortinet Firewalls and VPN Gateways

attack_patterns.name: Password Guessing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers in the FortiBleed campaign used automated tools to systematically attempt logins with lists of common or weak passwords against Fortinet management interfaces.

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: The primary technique used in the FortiBleed campaign, where threat actors used large dumps of usernames and passwords from unrelated breaches to gain access to Fortinet devices.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Once a valid credential pair was found through brute-force or credential stuffing, attackers used it to gain legitimate access to the network, making their initial activity appear as normal user behavior.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The attack surface for the FortiBleed campaign was the internet-facing login portals of Fortinet SSL VPNs and firewall management interfaces, which are designed for remote access.

campaigns.name: FortiBleed
campaigns.description: A large-scale credential harvesting campaign targeting organizations utilizing Fortinet firewalls and SSL VPN gateways by exploiting weak passwords and credential reuse.

identities.name: Fortinet
identities.description: Vendor of the FortiGate firewalls and SSL VPN gateways targeted by the FortiBleed credential harvesting campaign.

infrastructure.name: FortiBleed Attacker Server
infrastructure.description: An operational server used by the attackers in the FortiBleed campaign to conduct large-scale credential stuffing attacks.

indicators.name: FortiGate SSL VPN Failed Login
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiGate SSL VPN Failed Login
id: 2b1c3d4e-5f6a-4b9c-8d7e-6f5a4b3c2d1e
status: experimental
description: Detects a failed SSL VPN login attempt on a Fortinet FortiGate device. A high frequency of these events from a single source IP may indicate a brute-force or credential stuffing attack, such as those seen in the FortiBleed campaign.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/__sentry?ctype=balanced&uri=/vulnerabilities/global-cybersecurity-agencies-warn-of-credential-exposure-in-fortibleed-campaign-targeting-fortinet-firewalls-vpn-gateways/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1110.001
  - attack.t1110.004
  - attack.credential_access
level: low
logsource:
  product: fortinet
  service: fortigate
detection:
  selection:
    type: 'vpn'
    eventtype: 'ssl-vpn-login-fail'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects failed login attempts on FortiGate devices. A high rate of these events from a single source may indicate a brute-force or credential stuffing attack.

indicators.name: Anomalous FortiGate SSL VPN Login Page Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous FortiGate SSL VPN Login Page Access
id: 8a7b6c5d-4e3f-4a2b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to the default FortiGate SSL VPN login page URL path. While this can be legitimate, a high volume of requests from a single source IP, especially POST requests, can indicate a brute-force or credential stuffing attack like the FortiBleed campaign.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/__sentry?ctype=balanced&uri=/vulnerabilities/global-cybersecurity-agencies-warn-of-credential-exposure-in-fortibleed-campaign-targeting-fortinet-firewalls-vpn-gateways/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1133
  - attack.initial_access
level: informational
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-path: '/remote/login'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to the default FortiGate SSL VPN login page. High volumes of access from a single source can indicate brute-force attempts.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous FortiGate SSL VPN Login Page Access
Anomalous FortiGate SSL VPN Login Page Access
